game_name: Where in Time Is Carmen Sandiego?

element_introduction_order.mastery_context: player learns to navigate Chronoskimmer and interface before first case
player asks NPCs for travel clues at each location
player must deduce next destination from 4 options based on clue text
player collects descriptors to build accurate warrant
player identifies criminal preferences to construct warrant
every action consumes time budget; player must solve before expiration
visual confirmation of correct path choice; reinforces deduction accuracy
player compiles clues into arrest warrant with name, appearance, interests

element_introduction_order.element: Rank progression (Time Patroller → Super Time Sleuth)
element_introduction_order.mastery_context: promotions occur as player successfully captures criminals (80 total to beat game)
New American Desk Encyclopedia provided as reference for historical/geographical context
player must track Carmen herself as ultimate objective

typical_relief_triggers: arriving at correct location (VILE agent animation confirmation)
successfully deducing next destination from 4 options
typical_tension_triggers: time limit countdown active throughout case
choosing from 4 ambiguous travel options with incomplete information
multiple clues needed before warrant is complete
historical/geographical knowledge required to interpret clues correctly
rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: methodical investigation rhythm with moderate tension; time pressure creates steady anxiety while clue-gathering and location-hopping provide controlled relief cycles; educational pacing prioritizes comprehension over reflexive action

spatial_grammar.optional_content_signal: encyclopedia research available but optional; player can reference for deeper historical/geographical context

difficulty_curve.early_game: introductory criminal cases with simpler 4-option travel deductions; player learns clue interpretation mechanics; Time Cadet rank
difficulty_curve.mid_game: increasing complexity in criminal profiling; more nuanced clue interpretation required; historical periods and locations span wider range (400 AD to 1950s); rank progression through Time Patroller, Investigator, Inspector, Detective
difficulty_curve.late_game: 80 criminals accumulated; Ace Time Detective and Super Time Sleuth ranks; Carmen Sandiego as final target requiring synthesis of all learned mechanics
notable_spikes: transition from first case to second case (mechanics now fully independent)
Carmen Sandiego final case (elevated difficulty as ultimate adversary)

notable_level_patterns.description: each criminal case follows: NPC interrogation → travel clue receipt → destination deduction (4-option choice) → VILE agent confirmation → repeat until warrant components collected → arrest at correct location within time limit

criminal cases distributed across 1500 years of history (400 AD to 1950s); each era introduces different historical figures, events, inventions, and geographical contexts as reference material for clue interpretation
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Paul Revere horse theft case mentioned in reviews

notable_level_patterns.description: player must gather three complementary clue types: travel direction (next location), physical appearance (criminal identity), hobbies/interests (personality match) before warrant valid

sources_used: wikipedia: Where in Time Is Carmen Sandiego? (video game)
